trivia: A flying gas station older than the jet age's memory: the KC-135 first flew in 1956, and re-engined R models still anchor aerial refueling today. Few airframes anywhere have worked this long.
Descended from the same Dash 80 prototype as the Boeing 707, it is a living branch of the very first jetliner family tree.
funFacts: The R model swapped in CFM56 engines for a second life.
Many airframes are past 60 years of service.
